name: Sergio Eraso
title: PhD Student in Physics, MIT
bio: Sergio is a physicist working on nonequilibrium statistical physics and biophysics. He builds models, simulations, and tools to understand how simple local rules produce predictable large-scale behavior in complex systems.

name: Mehran Kardar
title: Francis Friedman Professor of Physics, MIT
bio: Professor Kardar is a theoretical physicist interested in a wide range of phenomena in statistical mechanics. He is particularly known for the Kardar–Parisi–Zhang (KPZ) equation in theoretical physics, which has been named after him and his two coauthors: the Nobel Prize laureate Giorgio Parisi, and Yi-Cheng Zhang.

name: Yariv Kafri
title: Professor of Physics, Technion — Israel Institute of Technology
bio: Professor Kafri is a theoretical physicist specializing in nonequilibrium statistical mechanics and active matter. His contributions range from non-equilibrium fluctuations and driven diffusive systems to translocation of polymers and the chemotaxis of bacteria.

name: Arup K. Chakraborty
title: John M. Deutch Institute Professor, MIT
bio: Professor Chakraborty is an American engineer and physicist focusing in biophysics, computational modeling and infectious disease.

name: Ruslan Medzhitov
title: Sterling Professor of Immunobiology, Yale University
bio: Professor Medzhitov studies the biology of inflammation, mechanisms of homoeostasis, allergic immunity and mechanisms of diseases.

name: Andriy Goychuk
title: Professor, Helmholtz Center for Infection Research
bio: Professor Goychuk is a physicist interested in the fundamental mechanisms underlying the self-organization of living matter.

name: David Goh
title: PhD Student, HST
bio: David is a PhD student at Harvard-MIT Health Sciences and Technology.

name: Ori Yehezkel
title: MSc Student, Technion — Israel Institute of Technology
bio: Ori is an MSc almunus from The Tenchion and is a former student of Yariv Kafri.
